tagline: A low-prestige extermination niche becomes the hidden maintenance layer that keeps a fantasy civilization from collapsing.
visualizationReason: Goblin Slayer is driven by dependency edges between frontier villages, guild bureaucracy, faith-casters, and specialist operators; the node graph best shows who supplies protection, who absorbs risk, and how trust links stabilize the system.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: He does not let anyone roll the dice.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_WARN]: FRONTIER THREAT UNDERPRICED — LOCAL FAILURE PROPAGATES TO REGIONAL INSTABILITY

introductionSummary: Goblin Slayer frames fantasy adventuring as a labor market where prestige quests attract talent while low-paying goblin contracts are ignored until villages are erased. The protagonist turns that neglected niche into a disciplined anti-goblin protocol built on preparation, terrain control, and cross-role party coordination. The series' core mechanics are less about flashy duels and more about risk management: finite miracles, chant latency, guild incentives, and community resilience under chronic asymmetric threat.

aiInsights.casual: Most adventurers chase fame, but Goblin Slayer focuses on the dangerous jobs nobody wants. By specializing completely, he prevents small goblin incidents from turning into mass-casualty disasters.
aiInsights.deep: Goblin Slayer models a mispriced security market: high-status actors allocate toward prestige threats while frontier micro-threats compound through rapid goblin reproduction and intelligence adaptation. The protagonist's doctrine is a throughput optimizer that converts preparation, reconnaissance, and low-cost tools into outsized casualty reduction. System stability depends on maintaining reliable node links among guild dispatch, rural reporting, and mixed-role party execution under strict resource ceilings.

powerSystem.name: Skill & Miracle Slot Economy
powerSystem.subtitle: Finite Daily Cast Budget
powerSystem.loreDesc: Priests and shamans can invoke only a limited number of miracles or spells before exhausting their divine and mental reserves.
powerSystem.systemDesc: Caster throughput is hard-capped per mission, forcing parties to allocate limited activations across scouting, control, and emergency defense windows.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Priestess must decide exactly when to spend Protection because every cast can be the difference between survival and wipe.

powerSystem.name: Preparation Doctrine
powerSystem.subtitle: Tools Over Heroics
powerSystem.loreDesc: Goblin Slayer relies on traps, smoke, mud, poison, and improvised weapons instead of dramatic fair fights.
powerSystem.systemDesc: Pre-combat planning converts uncertain encounters into controlled kill corridors with lower variance and reduced party attrition.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: He floods a nest and seals exits to force goblins into predictable choke points.

powerSystem.name: Guild Rank Signaling
powerSystem.subtitle: Credentialed Risk Routing
powerSystem.loreDesc: Adventurers are ranked from porcelain upward, and villages use those signals to request protection they can rarely afford.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A credential hierarchy routes labor toward perceived value, often starving low-reward extermination tasks of qualified responders.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: New porcelain teams accept goblin quests without operational literacy and suffer catastrophic losses.

powerSystem.name: Goblin Adaptive Ecology
powerSystem.subtitle: Parasitic Population Engine
powerSystem.loreDesc: Goblins learn from raids, copy weapons, and scale from cave pests to militarized warbands if left unchecked.
powerSystem.systemDesc: Unchecked reproduction plus tactical adaptation creates exponential local threat growth and recurring frontier collapse cycles.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: A single surviving goblin repeatedly reinfects cleared zones and rebuilds nest strength.

rankings.topTierLore: Mythic heroes handle dragon-scale and demon-lord crises beyond ordinary frontier incidents.
rankings.topTierSystem: Ultra-high throughput responders reserved for strategic threats, rarely allocated to local pest suppression.

tiers.name: Gold/Silver Specialists
tiers.loreDesc: Veteran parties capable of leading major subjugation campaigns and urban defense.
tiers.systemDesc: Reliable high-skill teams with enough coordination to execute multi-phase operations.

tiers.name: Bronze/Steel Core Workforce
tiers.loreDesc: The practical backbone of guild requests across towns, roads, and nearby ruins.
tiers.systemDesc: Mid-tier labor pool balancing volume and survivability for standard contracts.

tiers.name: Obsidian/Porcelain Novices
tiers.loreDesc: New adventurers with minimal field data, high optimism, and high casualty risk.
tiers.systemDesc: Low-experience entrants with weak threat models and limited coordination under pressure.

characters.name: Goblin Slayer
characters.title: Specialist Exterminator
characters.loreBio: A traumatized survivor who dedicates every waking hour to preventing goblin raids from repeating the tragedy of his childhood village.
characters.systemBio: Single-domain operator with extreme procedural discipline; transforms underfunded contracts into consistent threat-neutralization outcomes.
characters.signatureMoment: Uses the Gate Scroll ocean-pressure exploit to erase an entrenched nest that conventional force could not crack.

characters.name: Priestess
characters.title: Miracle Support Caster
characters.loreBio: A rookie cleric who survives her first party's annihilation and grows into a decisive tactical partner.
characters.systemBio: Low-latency support node whose limited miracle slots are allocated to barrier control, rescue timing, and mission continuity.
characters.signatureMoment: Recasts Protection creatively to crush goblins against stone and seal choke points.

characters.name: High Elf Archer
characters.title: Ranged Control Specialist
characters.loreBio: An elf ranger who initially mocks Goblin Slayer's grim methods, then recognizes their necessity in close-quarters extermination.
characters.systemBio: Precision ranged node that extends party engagement distance, recon quality, and anti-swarm containment.
characters.signatureMoment: Coordinates elevated firing lines to stop retreating goblin scouts from triggering reinforcements.

characters.name: Dwarf Shaman
characters.title: Earth & Utility Caster
characters.loreBio: A veteran shaman whose humor masks deep combat experience and battlefield adaptability.
characters.systemBio: Control-caster node that supplies terrain denial, enemy displacement, and emergency battlefield stabilization.
characters.signatureMoment: Uses shaping magic to collapse tunnel vectors and protect the party from flanking swarms.

characters.name: Lizard Priest
characters.title: Frontline Ritual Combatant
characters.loreBio: A reptilian priest-warrior who blends humor, honor, and brutal practicality in melee engagements.
characters.systemBio: Hybrid tank-support node that anchors formation integrity while adding summon utility and sustain pressure.
characters.signatureMoment: Holds a narrow corridor alone long enough for the team to complete a controlled purge.

characters.name: Guild Girl
characters.title: Dispatch Administrator
characters.loreBio: The Adventurer's Guild receptionist who quietly keeps goblin requests visible despite market disinterest.
characters.systemBio: Information-routing node that connects rural demand signals to available labor and preserves institutional memory of recurring threats.
characters.signatureMoment: Prioritizes urgent village reports so Goblin Slayer can intercept raids before full settlement collapse.

characters.name: Cow Girl
characters.title: Frontier Civilian Anchor
characters.loreBio: Goblin Slayer's childhood friend whose farm becomes an emotional and logistical safe zone after missions.
characters.systemBio: Civilian resilience node providing recovery bandwidth, social reintegration pressure, and local intelligence continuity.
characters.signatureMoment: Keeps him connected to ordinary life, preventing full psychological collapse into pure combat function.

characters.name: Sword Maiden
characters.title: Archbishop & Former Hero
characters.loreBio: A celebrated hero still haunted by goblin trauma who seeks Goblin Slayer's help when institutions hesitate.
characters.systemBio: High-authority religious node with broad legitimacy but localized vulnerability to unresolved threat memory and political constraints.
characters.signatureMoment: Commissions covert extermination of goblins beneath Water Town to avoid public panic and theological fracture.

characters.name: Spearman
characters.title: Frontier Vanguard Specialist
characters.loreBio: A veteran adventurer whose confidence and frontline discipline cover regions Goblin Slayer cannot personally patrol.
characters.systemBio: Parallel high-skill response node that prevents the ecosystem from overfitting to one specialist actor.
characters.signatureMoment: He holds high-threat fronts long enough for guild dispatches and civilian evacuation chains to stabilize.

characters.name: Witch
characters.title: Utility Caster & Recon Support
characters.loreBio: A composed caster who supports frontier veterans with precision utility and calm threat assessment.
characters.systemBio: Flexible support node that improves mission survivability by bridging reconnaissance, debuff timing, and extraction safety.
characters.signatureMoment: Her spell timing repeatedly converts uncertain skirmishes into controllable engagements for allied teams.

relationships.loreDesc: He trains her to survive by treating every corridor, sound, and corpse as tactical data.
relationships.systemDesc: Experience transfer edge that upgrades novice decision quality under high-lethality conditions.

relationships.loreDesc: She relies on his doctrine while gradually becoming confident enough to improvise within it.
relationships.systemDesc: Asymmetric trust link initially centered on specialist guidance and threat modeling.

relationships.loreDesc: Their friction over style evolves into mutual respect built on repeated survival.
relationships.systemDesc: Cross-domain coordination link between stealth-precision range and close-quarters extermination doctrine.

relationships.loreDesc: Dwarf Shaman reinforces Goblin Slayer's plans with earth-control utility and veteran judgment.
relationships.systemDesc: Support integration edge increasing trap reliability and denial-zone persistence.

relationships.loreDesc: Lizard Priest trusts his ruthless caution when others might overcommit to heroics.
relationships.systemDesc: Formation integrity edge that keeps frontline pressure stable during swarm encounters.

relationships.loreDesc: She depends on him to answer village requests that no one else accepts.
relationships.systemDesc: Dispatch-to-operator routing edge sustaining frontier incident response throughput.

relationships.loreDesc: Her steady support gives him a place to return to after each extermination.
relationships.systemDesc: Psychological stabilization edge that preserves long-term operational continuity.

relationships.loreDesc: Despite her status, she entrusts goblin-specific crises to the one specialist she believes can finish them.
relationships.systemDesc: Authority-to-specialist escalation edge used when institutional channels underperform.

relationships.loreDesc: He accepts her requests when they align with his mission to prevent further goblin casualties.
relationships.systemDesc: Conditional cooperation link bridging local extermination ops and city-level governance pressure.

relationships.loreDesc: He jokes about Goblin Slayer's obsession yet repeatedly backs him when stakes exceed routine quests.
relationships.systemDesc: Cross-team reinforcement edge that adds depth to regional response coverage.

relationships.loreDesc: Their battlefield trust lets them execute fast support-and-strike rotations under pressure.
relationships.systemDesc: Stable duo edge improving reliability of non-party interventions.

relationships.loreDesc: She routes urgent requests to available veterans when Goblin Slayer is already deployed elsewhere.
relationships.systemDesc: Dispatch redundancy edge preventing single-node saturation in guild operations.

factions.name: Adventurer's Guild
factions.loreDesc: A contract bureau matching civilians with armed talent, but vulnerable to prestige bias.
factions.systemDesc: Central labor allocator that optimizes for market incentives rather than worst-case frontier risk.

factions.name: Goblin Slayer Party
factions.loreDesc: A multi-race unit built around trust, discipline, and anti-goblin specialization.
factions.systemDesc: High-cohesion strike team tuned for cave warfare, attrition control, and casualty minimization.

factions.name: Goblin Warbands
factions.loreDesc: Adaptive raider nests that weaponize fear, numbers, and local surprise.
factions.systemDesc: Distributed parasitic clusters that exploit weak surveillance and delayed response loops.

factions.name: Temple Authority
factions.loreDesc: Religious institutions that provide healing legitimacy but are not always tactically proactive.
factions.systemDesc: Stability infrastructure with high symbolic capital and uneven tactical deployment speed.

factions.name: Frontier Villages and Militias
factions.loreDesc: Rural communities absorb first contact with goblin raids and often survive only through ad hoc local defense.
factions.systemDesc: Civilian early-warning and resilience layer whose reports determine whether response arrives before collapse.

rules.name: Never Underestimate Goblins
rules.subtitle: Threat Model Discipline
rules.loreConsequence: Treating goblins as weak fodder gets entire rookie parties killed.
rules.systemEquivalent: Underclassification of adaptive low-tier threats causes nonlinear casualty spikes.

rules.name: Spells Are Finite
rules.subtitle: Cast Budget Constraint
rules.loreConsequence: A wasted miracle can leave allies defenseless when the true ambush appears.
rules.systemEquivalent: Finite activation ceilings require deliberate timing and reserve management.
rules.loreSubtitle: Save miracles for the kill zone

rules.name: Terrain Dictates Survival
rules.subtitle: Battlefield Geometry
rules.loreConsequence: Caves, sewers, and ruins punish flashy swordplay and reward preparation.
rules.systemEquivalent: Constrained environments amplify setup advantages and invalidate generic duel doctrine.
rules.loreSubtitle: Control the tunnel, control the fight

rules.name: Frontier Reports Must Be Acted On Immediately
rules.subtitle: Early Response Window
rules.loreConsequence: Delays let small nests evolve into fortified breeding hubs.
rules.systemEquivalent: Slow incident response increases adversary reproduction and hardens future intervention cost.

rules.name: No Civilian-Scale Collateral
rules.subtitle: Containment Ethics
rules.loreConsequence: Using fire, flood, or poison recklessly can kill villagers and destroy livelihoods.
rules.systemEquivalent: Countermeasure selection is bounded by downstream social and infrastructure cost constraints.

rules.name: Prestige Markets Misprice Rural Threat
rules.loreSubtitle: The deadliest jobs are often the least glamorous
rules.systemSubtitle: Incentive mismatch creates recurring blind spots
rules.loreConsequence: High-visibility quests draw talent away while villages facing goblins remain under-protected until too late.
rules.systemEquivalent: Reward structures bias labor allocation, so low-pay extermination work requires intentional correction mechanisms.
rules.subtitle: Structural Escalation Constraint

counterplay.mechanic: Funnels mass numbers into narrow corridors where area denial and melee anchors can hold.
counterplay.loreDesc: Goblin Slayer turns their numerical advantage into a queue for slaughter.
counterplay.systemDesc: Geometry compression reduces parallel threat vectors and raises defender efficiency.

counterplay.mechanic: Frontline and ranged allies buy chant time for critical miracle deployment.
counterplay.loreDesc: Priestess survives by trusting teammates to guard her cast windows.
counterplay.systemDesc: Protection of vulnerable cast phases preserves high-impact utility output.

counterplay.mechanic: Eliminate leaders, eggs, and exits so no survivors rebuild the cycle.
counterplay.loreDesc: Half measures only guarantee another raid in a few weeks.
counterplay.systemDesc: Complete node neutralization prevents threat reseeding.

counterplay.mechanic: Dedicated goblin contracts maintain baseline security while elites chase major threats.
counterplay.loreDesc: One obsessive slayer fills the gap the hero economy ignores.
counterplay.systemDesc: Niche specialization patches incentive failures in broader task allocation.

counterplay.mechanic: Weaponized portal hydraulics overwhelm defenses impossible to breach by direct assault.
counterplay.loreDesc: A survival tool becomes a catastrophic anti-den strike.
counterplay.systemDesc: Environmental energy redirection bypasses conventional durability thresholds.

anomalies.name: Silver-Rank Single-Target Obsession
anomalies.ruleViolated: High-rank adventurers should diversify contracts for prestige and payout
anomalies.loreDesc: Goblin Slayer rejects fame loops to pursue only goblin extermination.
anomalies.systemDesc: Extreme specialization overrides market incentives and creates a unique reliability node.

anomalies.name: Defensive Miracle as Offensive Crusher
anomalies.ruleViolated: Barrier miracles are expected to be purely protective
anomalies.loreDesc: Priestess repurposes Protection into a lethal trapping technique.
anomalies.systemDesc: Function reinterpretation expands utility class from mitigation to kill-enablement.

anomalies.name: Ocean-Gate Weaponization
anomalies.ruleViolated: Teleportation tools are for escape, not siege-breaking firepower
anomalies.loreDesc: A retreat artifact becomes one of the deadliest anti-monster weapons in the setting.
anomalies.systemDesc: Cross-domain resource repurposing produces nonlinear offensive output from utility infrastructure.

anomalies.name: Single-Target Specialist Outperforms Heroic Generalists
anomalies.loreDesc: A supposedly narrow hunter repeatedly outperforms celebrated adventurers against the most persistent frontier threat.
anomalies.systemDesc: Domain-specialized optimization beats broad heroic profiles in environments dominated by one adaptive enemy class.
anomalies.ruleViolated: Generalist hero parties should outperform narrow specialists at scale

causalEvents.name: Village Massacre Trauma
causalEvents.trigger: Goblin raid destroys Goblin Slayer's childhood home
causalEvents.consequence: He commits to lifelong extermination doctrine and rejects prestige questing.
causalEvents.loreDesc: A survivor's vow becomes the foundation of his identity.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Personal catastrophic loss initializes permanent specialist role selection.

causalEvents.name: Rookie Party Catastrophe
causalEvents.trigger: Inexperienced adventurers accept a goblin quest without proper threat modeling
causalEvents.consequence: Priestess is rescued and joins Goblin Slayer's team, spreading his doctrine.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The cost of underestimating goblins is shown immediately and brutally.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Failure event recruits a high-retention support node into specialist network.

causalEvents.name: Water Town Infestation
causalEvents.trigger: Sword Maiden reports covert goblin activity beneath a major city
causalEvents.consequence: The party proves that even high-status regions are vulnerable to neglected micro-threats.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Urban sanctuaries are revealed to be fragile masks over unresolved fear.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Peripheral threat class penetrates central nodes when surveillance assumptions fail.

causalEvents.name: Farm Defense Operation
causalEvents.trigger: Organized goblin force targets Cow Girl's settlement
causalEvents.consequence: Community-level coordination and prepared defenses repel a mass raid.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The battle shows that villagers can survive when warned and organized.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Early warning plus layered defense converts civilian node from passive victim to resilient cluster.

causalEvents.name: Guild Training Grounds Raise Baseline Survival
causalEvents.trigger: Repeated goblin incidents force veterans to formalize anti-goblin methods for newer ranks.
causalEvents.consequence: More adventurers survive first-contact encounters, reducing catastrophic novice wipe patterns.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Hard lessons from massacres are converted into shared doctrine instead of being buried as isolated tragedies.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Knowledge codification shifts outcomes by moving tactics from individual memory to institutional protocol.

systemQuestions.question: Why is Goblin Slayer a node-graph universe instead of a pure combat ladder?
systemQuestions.answer: Because outcomes depend on who reports threats, who accepts contracts, and how trust links between guild, party, and civilians stay intact.

systemQuestions.question: What makes goblins systemically dangerous despite low individual strength?
systemQuestions.answer: Their rapid reproduction and adaptation mean small incidents compound quickly when response is delayed.

systemQuestions.question: Why do finite miracles change party strategy?
systemQuestions.answer: Limited cast slots force strict prioritization, so every support spell must be timed for maximum survival impact.

systemQuestions.question: How does the Adventurer's Guild create both stability and failure?
systemQuestions.answer: It routes contracts efficiently, but prestige-biased incentives under-serve low-paying goblin missions until specialists intervene.

systemQuestions.question: What is the core counterplay principle in Goblin Slayer?
systemQuestions.answer: Control terrain first, then force goblins into predictable kill zones where numbers stop mattering.
